reasoning: The informal statement is from Axler's "Linear Algebra Done Right" Exercise 5.11, where the book-wide context implicitly assumes V is finite-dimensional. Under that implicit assumption the statement is true and unambiguous. The CORRECTED formalization includes `[FiniteDimensional F V]`, while the ORIGINAL omits it. Without finite-dimensionality, the claim is false: a classical counterexample uses the right/left shift operators S, T on a countably-infinite-dimensional space where TS = I (so 0 is not an eigenvalue of TS) but ST has 0 as an eigenvalue. Thus the ORIGINAL is strictly stronger than the informal claim (it would imply it in the finite-dimensional case) but it is mathematically false in general. The error is a missing implicit finite-dimensionality premise that was assumed by context in Axler's book.
